topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on my doorstep, a glaring, unwelcome intrusion against the otherwise neat facade of my hallway. I stared at the shipping label, the handwriting unfamiliar and clearly not mine. It was addressed to 'A. Jenkins, Apt 3B.' My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck knotting with a slow, building tension. I picked up the package, the cheap paper crinkling under my grip. I took it back inside, the sound of the tape ripping echoing loudly in the silence of my apartment. What kind of inconsiderate behavior was this? I set it down on the kitchen counter, the gesture sharp and decisive, before pacing the length of the living room three times, my footsteps heavy and uneven.

[story 2]

I watched the delivery driver leave the package by the mail slot, a small, unassuming rectangle that represented an unnecessary complication. A low growl started in my chest, a vibration I could feel deep in my core. I walked over, my hands gripping the doorframe until my knuckles were white. When I finally wrestled the box inside, I didn't bother with polite unpacking. I tossed it onto the floor near the entryway with a careless thud. My breath came in short, sharp bursts. "Seriously?" I muttered to the empty room, the sound laced with a brittle edge. I started aggressively rifling through the contents, searching for some misplaced note, anything to explain this bizarre trespass.

[story 3]

It was just another Tuesday, and then *this* arrived. A package, clearly marked for the previous occupant of this unit. I picked it up, my fingers brushing against the rough texture of the exterior. A tight knot formed just beneath my sternum, a pressure that made my shoulders rise toward my ears. I dropped it onto the worn rug, the sound surprisingly loud. I stood motionless for a long moment, just observing the offending item. Then, with a sudden surge of irritation, I snatched it up again, turning it over and over in my hands, my gaze fixed on the shipping address as if it held the answer to a complex equation I couldn't solve. I threw it toward the recycling bin with unnecessary force.
